Title: Lineage-Specific CYP80 Expansion and Benzylisoquinoline Alkaloid Diversity in Early-Diverging Eudicots

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses dopamine and 4-hydroxyphenylacetaldehyde via a Pictet-Spengler reaction to form (S)-norcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/root hairs for many bisBIAs; aerial tissues for sinomenines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinally valuable specialized metabolites with structural diversity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of the central BIA skeleton precursor (S)-norcoclaurine and thus supports BIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; HPLC and LC-MS/MS product identification against authentic standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in stems and leaves; low in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in stems and leaves; low in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Different tissues: young leaf, leaf, young stem, stem, root hair, root
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting enzyme/committed entry step based on paper statement for NCS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only MdNCS6 among tested MdPR10/Bet v1 candidates showed detectable NCS activity; (S)-norcoclaurine accumulates mainly in roots/root hairs despite MdNCS6 expression being higher in aerial tissues, suggesting transport
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; targeted metabolomics; Figshare: 10.6084/m9.figshare.23509239
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point enzyme for engineering BIA production and for stacking with downstream tailoring enzymes to reconstruct Menispermum BIA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNCS6 was the only tested PR10/Bet v1 family member with confirmed NCS activity. 2. It catalyzes formation of (S)-norcoclaurine, the central precursor of BIAs. 3. Its aerial-biased expression contrasts with root accumulation of (S)-norcoclaurine, implying metabolite transport.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: (S)-reticuline-derived benzylisoquinoline alkaloids including bisBIAs, aporphines, sinomenines, and acutumines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/root hairs for bisBIAs and some aporphines; aerial tissues for sinomenines/acutumines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of the crucial precursor leading to (S)-reticuline and downstream diverse BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsomal enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High transcript abundance in both aerial tissues and roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High transcript abundance in both aerial tissues and roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Different tissues; heterologous transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hydroxylation activity confirmed both in yeast and tobacco transient expression; expression matches (S)-reticuline accumulation pattern
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; targeted metabolomics; Figshare: 10.6084/m9.figshare.23509239
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for reconstructing the central reticuline route in synthetic biology platforms for BIA production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCYP80B13 catalyzes C3′-hydroxylation of (S)-N-methylcoclaurine. 2. This step generates the crucial precursor to (S)-reticuline. 3. Activity was validated in yeast and Nicotiana benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dimerizes two molecules of (R)-N-methylcoclaurine via C3′-O-C4′ phenol coupling to form guattegaumerine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives specialized bisBIA biosynthesis by forming guattegaumerine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsomal enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots, root hairs, and stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots, root hairs, and stems
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Different tissues; heterologous transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among three tested MdCYP80Q genes, only MdCYP80Q12 was active; low identity to BsCYP80A1 suggests independent evolution
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; targeted metabolomics; Figshare: 10.6084/m9.figshare.23509239
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising gene for engineering root-enriched bisBIA formation in heterologous or native systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCYP80Q12 alone among tested MdCYP80Qs catalyzed bisBIA dimerization. 2. It forms (R,R)-guattegaumerine through C3′-O-C4′ phenol coupling. 3. Root/high root hair expression is consistent with bisBIA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C2′-C8 intramolecular phenol coupling of (S)-reticuline to form (S)-corytuberine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots for aporphines in M. dauricum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsomal enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in tissue expression survey; exact strongest tissue not explicitly emphasized
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in tissue expression survey; exact strongest tissue not explicitly emphasized
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Different tissues; heterologous transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accepts (S)-reticuline but not tested upstream substrates as productive substrates; shows stronger catalytic efficiency than MdCYP80G8P
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; targeted metabolomics; Figshare: 10.6084/m9.figshare.23509239
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to engineer aporphine branch flux from reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCYP80G8 catalyzes C2′-C8 phenol coupling of (S)-reticuline. 2. The product is (S)-corytuberine, an aporphine-type BIA intermediate/product. 3. Activity was validated in both yeast and tobacco transient assays.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereospecific C2′-C4a phenol coupling of (S)-reticuline to form sinoacutine, the enantiomeric counterpart of salutaridine-type chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates the Menispermaceae-specific sinomenine branch by converting (S)-reticuline into sinoacutine with stereospecificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsomal enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS with fragmentation; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate specificity assay with (R)-reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf-specific expression context; heterologous transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal branch determinant/key branch-switch enzyme for sinoacutine formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Novel leaf-specific enzyme; does not accept (R)-reticuline, indicating strict stereospecificity distinct from Papaver PsCYP719B1; supports independent evolution of sinomenine versus morphinan pathways
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; targeted metabolomics; Figshare: 10.6084/m9.figshare.23509239
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value branch enzyme for engineering sinomenine-related alkaloid production with stereochemical control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCYP80G10 is a newly discovered leaf-specific CYP80 enzyme. 2. It stereospecifically converts (S)-reticuline into sinoacutine through C2′-C4a phenol coupling. 3. It does not accept (R)-reticuline, supporting independent evolution of Menispermaceae sinomenine biosynthesis.
